mcq.q: What is the place value of the 7 in 374,562?
mcq.explain: The 7 is in the ten thousands place — 70,000.

mcq.q: Which number is the largest?
mcq.explain: 419,002 has 419 thousands — more than the others.

mcq.q: Five million, two hundred thousand in digits is…
mcq.explain: 5 millions + 200 thousands = 5,200,000.

mcq.q: What does the expanded form 600,000 + 40,000 + 800 + 3 equal?
Which number comes just after 199,999?
mcq.explain: Adding 1 to 199,999 gives 200,000.

mcq.q: Round 46,782 to the nearest thousand.
mcq.explain: The hundreds digit is 7, so round up to 47,000.

flashcards.back: The value of a digit depending on its position
A number written with digits, e.g., 3,452
Showing each digit's place value, e.g., 3,000 + 400 + 50 + 2
Writing a number in words, e.g., three thousand
Decide if a number is greater than, less than, or equal to another
Replace a number with one close by that is easier to work with

meta.hook: Time to play with numbers! Number Explorers

lessons.text: Time to play with numbers! Number Explorers

mcq.q: A set is…
choices: A group of objects that share something
mcq.explain: A set is a well-defined collection of objects that share a property.

mcq.q: Which of these items belongs to the set of Jamaican fruits?
mcq.explain: Ackee is Jamaica's national fruit.

mcq.q: How do we show a set in writing?
mcq.explain: We use curly braces, e.g., {1, 2, 3}.

mcq.q: Which set has no members?
mcq.explain: An empty set (or null set) has zero elements — written { } or Ø.

mcq.q: Set A = {2, 4, 6}. Set B = {4, 6, 8}. What is A ∩ B (what's in BOTH)?
mcq.explain: Intersection: elements that appear in both sets — 4 and 6.

mcq.q: Classifying means…
mcq.explain: Classifying is sorting items into groups based on shared properties.

flashcards.back: A group of objects sharing a property
A set with no members { } or Ø
All elements from both sets combined
Only elements that are in both sets
A set whose members are all inside another set
Overlapping circles that show how sets relate
The set of all possible elements being considered

meta.hook: Time to play with numbers! Sets & Classification

lessons.text: Time to play with numbers! Sets & Classification

mcq.q: A factor of a number is…
choices: A number it divides into evenly
mcq.explain: A factor divides a number with no remainder. E.g., 3 is a factor of 12.

mcq.q: Which is a multiple of 6?
mcq.explain: 18 = 6 × 3, so 18 is a multiple of 6.

mcq.q: What are the factors of 12?
A prime number has exactly…
mcq.explain: A prime has exactly 2 factors: 1 and itself. E.g., 7 = 1 × 7.

mcq.q: Which of these is a prime number?
mcq.explain: 17 only has factors 1 and 17.

mcq.q: What is the LCM (Lowest Common Multiple) of 4 and 6?
mcq.explain: Multiples of 4: 4, 8, 12, 16... Multiples of 6: 6, 12, 18... The lowest in common is 12.

flashcards.back: A number that divides another evenly
The result of multiplying a number by any whole number
A number with exactly 2 factors: 1 and itself
A number with more than 2 factors
Highest Common Factor — biggest factor two numbers share
Lowest Common Multiple — smallest multiple two numbers share
Writing a number as a product of primes
Whether one number divides another with no remainder

meta.hook: Time to play with numbers! Factors & Multiples

lessons.text: Time to play with numbers! Factors & Multiples

mcq.q: In the fraction 3/5, which number is the denominator?
mcq.explain: The bottom number (5) is the denominator — the total number of equal parts.

mcq.q: Which fraction is equivalent to 1/2?
mcq.explain: 3/6 simplifies to 1/2.

mcq.q: Which is larger: 3/4 or 2/3?
What is 1/4 + 2/4?
mcq.explain: Same denominator — add the numerators: 1 + 2 = 3, giving 3/4.

mcq.q: If Kemar ate 3/8 of a bulla cake and Keisha ate 2/8, how much was eaten?
mcq.explain: 3/8 + 2/8 = 5/8 of the bulla cake.

mcq.q: A mixed number looks like…
mcq.explain: A mixed number has a whole part and a fraction part, e.g., 3 1/2.

flashcards.back: Different fractions that equal the same value
Divide top and bottom by the same number until smallest form
The same bottom number — needed to add/subtract fractions

meta.hook: Time to play with numbers! Fraction Champions

lessons.text: Time to play with numbers! Fraction Champions

mcq.q: The decimal 0.5 is the same as…
mcq.explain: 0.5 means 5 tenths, which equals 1/2.

mcq.q: Which decimal is largest?
mcq.explain: 0.77 is larger than 0.707, 0.7, and 0.07.

mcq.q: In 34.56, what does the 6 represent?
mcq.explain: The 6 is two places after the decimal, so it's 6 hundredths.
Two tenths plus three tenths = five tenths = 0.5.

mcq.q: Which is J$75.50 written differently?
mcq.explain: 0.50 after the dollar = 50 cents.

mcq.q: Round 3.67 to the nearest tenth.
mcq.explain: Hundredths digit (7) rounds the tenths up, giving 3.7.

flashcards.back: Separates whole numbers from parts smaller than 1
Look at next digit — 5 or more rounds up

meta.hook: Time to play with numbers! Decimal Adventures

lessons.text: Time to play with numbers! Decimal Adventures

mcq.q: Round 58 to the nearest ten.
mcq.explain: Ones digit is 8 (≥5), so round up to 60.

mcq.q: Round 342 to the nearest hundred.
mcq.explain: Tens digit is 4 (<5), so round down to 300.

mcq.q: Round 4,567 to the nearest thousand.
mcq.explain: Hundreds digit is 5, so round up to 5,000.

mcq.q: Round 12.34 to the nearest whole number.
mcq.explain: Tenths digit is 3 (<5), so round down to 12.

mcq.q: Why do we round numbers?
mcq.explain: Rounding helps us estimate quickly when exact numbers aren't needed.

mcq.q: Which is the rule for rounding?
choices: 5 or more → round up; 4 or less → round down
mcq.explain: 5-or-more rule: look at the digit to the right; if ≥5 round up, if <5 round down.

flashcards.back: Increase the rounding digit by 1 when next digit is 5 or more
Keep the rounding digit the same when next digit is 4 or less
A careful guess using rounded numbers
The digit you are rounding to
The digit just to the right of the rounding digit

meta.hook: Time to play with numbers! Rounding Experts

lessons.text: Time to play with numbers! Rounding Experts

mcq.q: What is 48 ÷ 6?
In 24 ÷ 4 = 6, what is the dividend?
mcq.explain: The dividend is the number being divided (24).

mcq.q: A teacher has 56 pencils to share among 8 students equally. Each gets…
mcq.explain: 56 ÷ 8 = 7 pencils each.

mcq.q: What is 100 ÷ 4?

flashcards.back: A method: divide, multiply, subtract, bring down
Division means splitting into equal groups

meta.hook: Time to play with numbers! Division Challenges

lessons.text: Time to play with numbers! Division Challenges

mcq.q: How many minutes in an hour?
mcq.explain: 60 minutes make 1 hour.

mcq.q: 2:30 pm in 24-hour time is…
mcq.explain: Add 12 to any pm time: 2 + 12 = 14, so 14:30.

mcq.q: From 9:15 am to 11:45 am is…
mcq.explain: 9:15 → 11:15 is 2 hours; plus 30 minutes = 2 hours 30 minutes.

mcq.q: How many seconds in 5 minutes?
mcq.explain: 5 × 60 = 300 seconds.

mcq.q: School starts at 8:00 am and ends at 2:30 pm. How long is the school day?
mcq.explain: 8:00 to 2:30 = 6 hours 30 minutes.

mcq.q: What does 'quarter to 4' mean?
mcq.explain: Quarter to 4 = 15 minutes before 4 = 3:45.

meta.hook: Time to play with numbers! Time Travelers

lessons.text: Time to play with numbers! Time Travelers

mcq.q: How many centimetres in a metre?
mcq.explain: 1 metre = 100 centimetres.

mcq.q: How many grams in a kilogram?
A 2-litre bottle of Jamaican ginger beer holds… millilitres.
Which is the best unit to measure a person's height?
mcq.explain: Centimetres (or metres) are best for height.

mcq.q: How many kilometres in 3,000 metres?
A bag of rice weighs 5 kg. In grams that is…

meta.hook: Time to play with numbers! Measurement Masters

lessons.text: Time to play with numbers! Measurement Masters

mcq.q: Perimeter of a rectangle 5 cm by 3 cm is…
mcq.explain: Perimeter = 2(5 + 3) = 16 cm.

mcq.q: Area of the same rectangle (5 cm × 3 cm) is…
mcq.explain: Area = length × width = 5 × 3 = 15 square cm.

mcq.q: A square has sides of 7 cm. What is its perimeter?
A square has sides of 7 cm. What is its area?
mcq.explain: Side × side = 7 × 7 = 49 cm².

mcq.q: What is the unit for area?
mcq.explain: Area is always in square units (cm², m², etc.).

mcq.q: A rectangular garden is 8 m long and 4 m wide. What area needs grass seed?

flashcards.back: Amount of surface a shape covers
cm², m², km² — used for area
cm, m, km — used for perimeter

meta.hook: Time to play with numbers! Area & Perimeter

lessons.text: Time to play with numbers! Area & Perimeter

mcq.q: A triangle has how many sides?
mcq.explain: Tri- means three. A triangle has 3 sides and 3 angles.

mcq.q: A shape with 8 sides is a…
mcq.explain: Octo- means 8. Octagons have 8 sides — like a stop sign.

mcq.q: An angle that is exactly 90° is called…
mcq.explain: A right angle is 90° — like the corner of a book.

mcq.q: An angle less than 90° is…
mcq.explain: Acute angles are small and sharp — less than 90°.

mcq.q: A 3D shape with 6 square faces is a…
mcq.explain: A cube has 6 equal square faces — like a die.

mcq.q: How many edges does a rectangular prism have?
mcq.explain: A rectangular prism (box) has 12 edges — 4 on top, 4 on bottom, 4 vertical.

flashcards.back: A closed shape with straight sides

meta.hook: Time to play with numbers! Geometry World

lessons.text: Time to play with numbers! Geometry World

mcq.q: A chart that uses bars to show data is called a…
mcq.explain: Bar graphs use rectangular bars of different lengths.

mcq.q: A pictograph uses…
mcq.explain: Pictographs use pictures/icons to represent data amounts.

mcq.q: The most common value in a data set is the…
mcq.explain: Mode = the value that appears most often.

mcq.q: What is the mean of 2, 4, 6, 8?
The difference between the highest and lowest values is the…
mcq.explain: Range = highest − lowest.

mcq.q: Probability of rolling a 6 on a regular die is…
mcq.explain: One favourable outcome (6) out of 6 possible outcomes = 1/6.

flashcards.back: Chart using bars to compare data
Chart using pictures to show data
Chart using a line to show change over time

meta.hook: Time to play with numbers! Data Detectives

lessons.text: Time to play with numbers! Data Detectives

mcq.q: Which food group gives us energy quickly?
mcq.explain: Carbohydrates (rice, bread, yam) provide quick energy.

mcq.q: Which is a good Jamaican source of protein?
mcq.explain: Ackee and saltfish contain protein — saltfish is especially rich.

mcq.q: Vitamins are found in…
mcq.explain: Fruits and vegetables like callaloo and mango are rich in vitamins.

mcq.q: Which nutrient helps build strong bones and teeth?
mcq.explain: Calcium, found in milk and cheese, builds strong bones and teeth.

mcq.q: Too much of which can cause tooth decay?
mcq.explain: Sugar feeds bacteria in the mouth, which damages teeth.

mcq.q: How many glasses of water should a child drink daily?
mcq.explain: Around 6–8 glasses keeps the body hydrated.

flashcards.back: Stores energy (oil, butter) — needed in small amounts
Keeps body systems working (from fruits & veg)
Eating the right amounts from all food groups

meta.hook: Let's explore: Nutrition & Health

lessons.text: Let's explore: Nutrition & Health

mcq.q: What is at the start of every food chain?
mcq.explain: The sun provides energy that plants use to make food. Energy flow begins there.

mcq.q: An animal that eats only plants is a…
mcq.explain: Herbivores eat only plants — like cows and goats.

mcq.q: In a food web, arrows point from…
mcq.explain: Arrows show energy flow — from prey to predator.

mcq.q: Which is a decomposer?
mcq.explain: Fungi and bacteria break down dead matter.

mcq.q: Grass → Goat → Mongoose. The mongoose is a…
mcq.explain: Goat eats grass (primary); mongoose eats goat (secondary consumer).

mcq.q: If all plants were removed, what would happen?
mcq.explain: Plants are the base of the food chain — without them, everything collapses.

meta.hook: Let's explore: Food Chains & Webs

lessons.text: Let's explore: Food Chains & Webs

mcq.q: Which is a major staple food grown in Jamaica?
mcq.explain: Yam is a major staple grown across Jamaica — Trelawny is famous for it.

mcq.q: Farmers need what to grow good crops?
mcq.explain: Good soil, water, and sunlight are basic needs for crops.

mcq.q: Jamaica's Blue Mountain region is famous for growing…
mcq.explain: Blue Mountain Coffee is world-famous, grown at high altitude.

mcq.q: Crop rotation helps by…
mcq.explain: Rotating crops replenishes different nutrients in the soil.

mcq.q: A disease that affected Jamaican banana crops is…
mcq.explain: Black sigatoka is a fungal disease that damages banana leaves.

mcq.q: Organic farming does NOT use…
mcq.explain: Organic farming avoids synthetic pesticides and fertilisers.

flashcards.back: Main food of a people (yam, rice, bread)
Crop grown mainly to be sold (coffee, sugar)
Food for plants added to soil
Natural fertiliser made from rotted plant matter
Changing which crop grows in a field each season
Animal or insect that damages crops

meta.hook: Let's explore: Growing Our Food

lessons.text: Let's explore: Growing Our Food

mcq.q: Which instrument measures wind direction?
mcq.explain: A wind vane points into the wind to show direction.

mcq.q: A rain gauge measures…
mcq.explain: A rain gauge collects rain to measure how much falls.

mcq.q: Jamaica's hurricane season is roughly…
mcq.explain: Atlantic hurricane season runs June 1 to November 30.

mcq.q: Humidity means…
mcq.explain: Humidity is the amount of water vapour in the air — high in Jamaica.

mcq.q: Cumulus clouds look like…
mcq.explain: Cumulus = puffy, cotton-ball clouds in fair weather.

mcq.q: A barometer measures…
mcq.explain: Air pressure changes signal weather changes.

flashcards.back: Long-term weather pattern of a place

meta.hook: Let's explore: Weather Station

lessons.text: Let's explore: Weather Station

mcq.q: A force is…
mcq.explain: Forces are pushes or pulls that can change motion or shape.

mcq.q: Friction slows things down because…
mcq.explain: Friction happens when two surfaces rub together.

mcq.q: Gravity pulls objects…
mcq.explain: Gravity pulls everything toward Earth's centre.

mcq.q: Which surface has the LEAST friction?
mcq.explain: Wet ice is slippery — very low friction.

mcq.q: When you push a shopping cart and it moves, force has caused…
mcq.explain: Force can change an object's motion.

mcq.q: A magnet attracts…
mcq.explain: Magnets attract magnetic materials like iron, steel, and nickel.

flashcards.back: Force that opposes motion between surfaces
Force that pulls objects toward Earth
Force between magnets or magnetic materials
Friction from air pushing against a moving object
Equal forces that don't change motion
Forces that cause motion to change

meta.hook: Let's explore: Forces in Action

lessons.text: Let's explore: Forces in Action

mcq.q: Energy from the sun is called…
mcq.explain: Solar = from the sun. Jamaica gets lots of sunshine — good for solar panels.

mcq.q: A flashlight changes chemical energy (battery) into…
mcq.explain: Batteries produce electrical energy → light energy (and a little heat).

mcq.q: Which is a renewable energy source?
mcq.explain: Wind is renewable — it never runs out.

mcq.q: A moving object has what type of energy?
mcq.explain: Kinetic energy = energy of motion.

mcq.q: When we turn on a lamp, electrical energy becomes…
mcq.explain: Electrical → light (and heat from the bulb).

mcq.q: Jamaica's major non-renewable fuel is…
mcq.explain: Jamaica imports oil for most electricity generation.

flashcards.back: Can be replaced naturally (sun, wind, water)

meta.hook: Let's explore: Energy Everywhere

lessons.text: Let's explore: Energy Everywhere

mcq.q: A simple machine that lifts loads using a rope and wheel is a…
mcq.explain: A pulley uses a wheel and rope to change the direction of force.

mcq.q: A seesaw is an example of a…
mcq.explain: A seesaw is a first-class lever with the pivot in the middle.

mcq.q: A ramp or slope used to lift things is an…
mcq.explain: An inclined plane reduces the force needed by spreading it over a longer distance.

mcq.q: Scissors combine which two simple machines?
mcq.explain: Scissors are two levers joined, each blade being a wedge.

mcq.q: Simple machines make work…
mcq.explain: They reduce the effort needed or change the direction of the force.

mcq.q: A screw is like a long…
choices: Inclined plane wrapped around a rod
mcq.explain: A screw is an inclined plane spiralling around a cylinder.

flashcards.back: Tool with few parts that makes work easier
A rigid bar that pivots on a fulcrum
Wheel with a rope used to lift loads
Round wheel attached to a rod
Inclined plane wrapped around a rod
Two or more simple machines together

meta.hook: Let's explore: Amazing Machines

lessons.text: Let's explore: Amazing Machines

mcq.q: The pump that moves blood around the body is the…
mcq.explain: The heart pumps blood continuously through the circulatory system.

mcq.q: Lungs are mainly used to…
mcq.explain: Lungs take in oxygen and release carbon dioxide.

mcq.q: The digestive system starts in the…
mcq.explain: Chewing and saliva in the mouth begin digestion.

mcq.q: Bones that protect the brain form the…
mcq.explain: The skull is a hard bony case that protects the brain.

mcq.q: Muscles connect to bones by…
mcq.explain: Tendons attach muscles to bones so we can move.

mcq.q: The body's control centre is the…
mcq.explain: The brain sends and receives signals that control the whole body.

flashcards.back: Mouth → stomach → intestines — breaks down food

meta.hook: Let's explore: Body as Machine

lessons.text: Let's explore: Body as Machine

mcq.q: Good public speakers…
mcq.explain: Clear volume and good eye contact help the audience understand.

mcq.q: Before you speak, you should…
mcq.explain: Planning makes your message organised and confident.

mcq.q: Eye contact with your audience is…
mcq.explain: It connects you to listeners and keeps them engaged.

mcq.q: 'Um' and 'ah' are called…
mcq.explain: Filler words distract the audience — pause instead.

mcq.q: A good speaker's tone should be…
mcq.explain: Varying tone keeps listeners interested and shows emotion.

mcq.q: When giving a speech in Jamaica about your home parish, you should…
mcq.explain: Facts and stories make a speech convincing.

flashcards.back: How loud or soft you speak
How fast or slow you speak
How clearly you say each word
Hand and body movements that support speech

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Speaking Champions

lessons.text: Words are powerful! Speaking Champions

mcq.q: Active listening means…
mcq.explain: It is full attention with the aim of understanding.

mcq.q: A good listener uses…
mcq.explain: Looking, hearing and thinking together = active listening.

mcq.q: You should NOT do what while listening?
mcq.explain: Using the phone distracts you and disrespects the speaker.

mcq.q: Reasons we listen include…
mcq.explain: We listen for many purposes — information, enjoyment, learning, directions.

mcq.q: A summary of a speaker's main idea is a…
mcq.explain: Paraphrasing uses your own words to repeat the key idea.

mcq.q: When teacher Miss Brown gives multi-step directions, you should…
mcq.explain: Writing notes helps you remember complex instructions.

flashcards.back: Facts that back up the main idea
A guess based on what is said and not said

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Active Listeners

lessons.text: Words are powerful! Active Listeners

mcq.q: A synonym is a word that…
mcq.explain: Synonyms share meaning: big/large, happy/joyful.

mcq.q: An antonym for 'hot' is…
mcq.explain: Antonyms mean the opposite — hot/cold.

mcq.q: A prefix goes…
choices: At the end of a word
At the start of a word
mcq.explain: Prefix = front: un-happy, re-do, pre-view.

mcq.q: What does the suffix '-ful' mean in 'beautiful'?
mcq.explain: -ful = 'full of'. Beautiful = full of beauty.

mcq.q: Homophones are words that…
mcq.explain: Like 'their/there/they're' — same sound, different meaning.

mcq.q: 'Patois' in Jamaica is a…
mcq.explain: Patois is Jamaica's everyday creole language.

flashcards.back: Letters added to the start of a word
Letters added to the end of a word
The base part of a word
Book with word meanings and spellings

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Word Wizards

lessons.text: Words are powerful! Word Wizards

mcq.q: The main idea of a text is…
mcq.explain: Main idea = the most important point the text makes.

mcq.q: Inferences are made from…
choices: Clues plus what you already know
mcq.explain: Inference = reading between the lines using evidence and prior knowledge.

mcq.q: A 'conclusion' is…
mcq.explain: A conclusion is what you decide based on the information given.

mcq.q: The 'who, what, where, when, why' questions help you find…
mcq.explain: 5Ws help locate key facts in any text.

mcq.q: If a paragraph says 'Dunn's River Falls is in St. Ann' and the next says it is 55m tall, what is the main idea?
choices: The falls are in St. Ann and 55m tall
mcq.explain: The main idea combines the key facts stated.

mcq.q: Skimming means…
mcq.explain: Skimming is fast reading to catch the gist.

flashcards.back: What the text is mostly about
A fact that supports the main idea
Use clues + knowledge to figure out meaning
Decide based on what you read
Read quickly to get the gist
Nearby words that hint at meaning

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Reading Detectives

lessons.text: Words are powerful! Reading Detectives

mcq.q: A dictionary gives you…
mcq.explain: Dictionaries list words with meanings, pronunciations, and parts of speech.

mcq.q: To find information on the internet, you use a…
mcq.explain: Search engines like Google help you find websites.

mcq.q: A book with maps is called a(n)…
mcq.explain: An atlas is a collection of maps.

mcq.q: A thesaurus helps you find…
mcq.explain: A thesaurus lists words with similar/opposite meanings.

mcq.q: Which is a reliable source for a school project?
mcq.explain: Library books and government sites are checked for accuracy.

mcq.q: What is the Table of Contents?
choices: A list at the start showing chapters and pages
mcq.explain: The Table of Contents tells you what's in the book and where.

flashcards.back: Alphabetical list at the back of a book

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Information Hunters

lessons.text: Words are powerful! Information Hunters

mcq.q: A noun is…
choices: A person, place, thing or idea
mcq.explain: Kingston, ackee, school, friendship — all nouns.

mcq.q: A verb shows…
mcq.explain: Verbs show action (run) or state (is, seems).

mcq.q: An adjective describes a…
mcq.explain: Adjectives tell us more about nouns: a TALL tree.

mcq.q: Choose the pronoun: 'Maya gave ___ a gift.'
mcq.explain: Pronouns replace nouns — 'her' takes the place of a name.

mcq.q: An adverb often ends in…
mcq.explain: Quickly, happily, silently — adverbs often end in -ly.

mcq.q: Which sentence is correct?
mcq.explain: Third-person singular 'she' takes the verb form 'goes'.

flashcards.back: Replaces a noun (he, she, it, they)
Joins words or clauses (and, but, or)

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Grammar Masters

lessons.text: Words are powerful! Grammar Masters

mcq.q: The first step in writing is to…
mcq.explain: Planning your ideas first leads to clearer writing.

mcq.q: A topic sentence is…
choices: The main idea of a paragraph
mcq.explain: It tells the reader what the paragraph is about.

mcq.q: A story usually has…
mcq.explain: Stories follow a clear structure: beginning, middle, end.

mcq.q: 'Editing' means…
mcq.explain: Editing fixes errors to polish the writing.

mcq.q: A paragraph usually has how many sentences?
mcq.explain: Paragraphs usually have 3–5 or more related sentences.

mcq.q: When writing about Jamaica's Independence Day, a good opening could be…
choices: 'On August 6, 1962, Jamaica celebrated its birth as a free nation.'
mcq.explain: Opens with a specific, interesting fact tied to the topic.

flashcards.back: Thinking and jotting ideas before writing

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Writing Workshop

lessons.text: Words are powerful! Writing Workshop

mcq.q: A poem that does not rhyme is called…
mcq.explain: Free verse has no strict rhyme or metre.

mcq.q: A simile uses the word…
mcq.explain: Similes compare using 'like' or 'as' (brave as a lion).

mcq.q: A metaphor says something IS something else. Example:
choices: The sea is a blue blanket
mcq.explain: A metaphor makes a direct comparison without 'like/as'.

mcq.q: Personification gives…
mcq.explain: E.g., 'The wind whispered' — wind doesn't really whisper.

mcq.q: 'Buzz' and 'boom' are examples of…
mcq.explain: Onomatopoeia — words that sound like what they mean.

mcq.q: 'Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers' is…
mcq.explain: Repeated P sounds — alliteration.

flashcards.back: Words that sound like their meaning

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Creative Expression

lessons.text: Words are powerful! Creative Expression

mcq.q: Which is a way to communicate?
mcq.explain: Speech, writing, and body language all communicate.

mcq.q: Polite greeting in Jamaica includes…
mcq.explain: A polite greeting is respectful and starts communication well.

mcq.q: When you disagree respectfully, you should…
mcq.explain: Share your view politely with a reason.

mcq.q: A formal letter to a principal begins with…
mcq.explain: Formal letters use respectful salutations.

mcq.q: Which is non-verbal communication?
mcq.explain: Non-verbal = without words (face, gestures, body language).

mcq.q: Sending a text message is an example of…
mcq.explain: Text = written communication.

flashcards.back: The one who sends the message
The one who gets the message

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Communication Skills

lessons.text: Words are powerful! Communication Skills

mcq.q: Jamaican Patois is a…
mcq.explain: Patois (Jamaican Creole) blends English with African languages.

mcq.q: 'Mi deh yah' in Patois means…
mcq.explain: A common Patois greeting answer — 'I am here / I'm good.'

mcq.q: Standard English is mostly used in…
mcq.explain: Standard English is used in school, work, news, and formal writing.

mcq.q: Folk stories with Anancy teach us about…
mcq.explain: Anancy stories are West African-rooted tales that teach lessons through a clever spider.

mcq.q: Being bilingual in Jamaica means you speak…
mcq.explain: Many Jamaicans switch between Patois and Standard English.

mcq.q: What's one benefit of speaking two languages?
choices: Easier to connect with more people
mcq.explain: Bilingualism widens communication and culture.

flashcards.back: A blended language of two or more

meta.hook: Words are powerful! Language & Culture

lessons.text: Words are powerful! Language & Culture

mcq.q: Who were the first people of Jamaica?
mcq.explain: The Tainos (Arawaks) were the indigenous people when Columbus arrived in 1494.

mcq.q: Christopher Columbus reached Jamaica in…
mcq.explain: Columbus landed in Jamaica on his second voyage in 1494.

mcq.q: Who ruled Jamaica BEFORE the British?
mcq.explain: Spain controlled Jamaica from 1494 to 1655.

mcq.q: The British captured Jamaica from Spain in…
mcq.explain: Britain took Jamaica in 1655 and held it until independence.

mcq.q: Tainos lived mainly by…
mcq.explain: Tainos farmed cassava, fished, and hunted small animals.

mcq.q: Disease brought by Europeans devastated the Tainos because…
mcq.explain: Smallpox and other diseases wiped out most of the Taino population.

flashcards.back: Taino name for Jamaica — 'land of wood and water'
Spanish system forcing Tainos to work

meta.hook: A story from our world: European Arrival

lessons.text: A story from our world: European Arrival

mcq.q: Enslaved Africans were brought to Jamaica to work on…
mcq.explain: Sugar was Jamaica's main crop; enslaved Africans were forced to grow and process it.

mcq.q: The journey across the Atlantic was called the…
mcq.explain: The Middle Passage was the brutal sea journey from Africa to the Americas.

mcq.q: Enslaved people who escaped and formed free communities were called…
mcq.explain: Maroons lived free in the mountains and defended their freedom fiercely.

mcq.q: Nanny of the Maroons is remembered as…
choices: A warrior queen and National Hero
mcq.explain: Queen Nanny led Windward Maroons against the British.

mcq.q: Slavery in the British Empire ended in…
mcq.explain: The Emancipation Act freed enslaved people on August 1, 1834.

mcq.q: Plantations grew mainly…
mcq.explain: Sugar cane was Jamaica's main export crop during slavery.

flashcards.back: Large estate growing a cash crop (sugar)
Escaped enslaved person in free community

meta.hook: A story from our world: Slavery & Plantation

lessons.text: A story from our world: Slavery & Plantation

mcq.q: Sam Sharpe led the…
mcq.explain: Sam Sharpe led the 1831 Baptist War, which sped up emancipation.

mcq.q: Paul Bogle led the…
mcq.explain: Paul Bogle led the 1865 Morant Bay Rebellion demanding justice.

mcq.q: Apprenticeship ended in…
mcq.explain: Full emancipation came in 1838 when apprenticeship ended.

mcq.q: The Morant Bay Rebellion was sparked by…
mcq.explain: Former slaves demanded land, fair wages, and justice.

mcq.q: George William Gordon is honoured for…
choices: Fighting for the poor and supporting Bogle
mcq.explain: Gordon was a preacher/politician executed for supporting Bogle's cause.

mcq.q: Jamaica became independent on…
mcq.explain: Jamaica gained independence from Britain on August 6, 1962.

meta.hook: A story from our world: Fight for Freedom

lessons.text: A story from our world: Fight for Freedom

mcq.q: Jamaica has how many National Heroes?
mcq.explain: Seven: Nanny, Sharpe, Bogle, Gordon, Garvey, Bustamante, Manley.

mcq.q: Marcus Garvey taught 'Up you mighty race!' to…
choices: Uplift Africans and people of African descent
mcq.explain: Garvey promoted Black pride, self-reliance, and pan-Africanism.

mcq.q: National Heroes Day is celebrated on…
mcq.explain: Jamaica honours its heroes on the third Monday in October.

mcq.q: Norman Manley founded which party?
mcq.explain: Norman Manley co-founded the People's National Party (PNP).

mcq.q: Alexander Bustamante founded…
mcq.explain: Bustamante founded the Jamaica Labour Party and the Bustamante Industrial Trade Union.

mcq.q: Queen Nanny is the only…
mcq.explain: Queen Nanny is the only female among Jamaica's seven National Heroes.

meta.hook: A story from our world: National Heroes

lessons.text: A story from our world: National Heroes

mcq.q: Jamaica is divided into how many parishes?
mcq.explain: Jamaica has 14 parishes.

mcq.q: Kingston is the…
mcq.explain: Kingston is Jamaica's capital and main port city.

mcq.q: The head of local parish government is called the…
mcq.explain: The mayor leads the Municipal Corporation (parish council).

mcq.q: The head of Jamaica's government is the…
mcq.explain: The Prime Minister leads Jamaica's government.

mcq.q: The Queen/King is represented in Jamaica by the…
mcq.explain: The Governor-General represents the British monarch as Head of State.

mcq.q: Parish councillors are chosen by…
mcq.explain: Voters elect councillors every few years.

flashcards.back: Government by the people, through voting

meta.hook: A story from our world: Parish Government

lessons.text: A story from our world: Parish Government

mcq.q: CARICOM is a group of…
mcq.explain: The Caribbean Community (CARICOM) promotes economic and political cooperation.

mcq.q: Reggae music originated in…
mcq.explain: Reggae grew out of ska and rocksteady in late-1960s Jamaica.

mcq.q: Which is a Caribbean Carnival dance?
mcq.explain: Soca is Trinidad's Carnival music/dance.

mcq.q: Bob Marley is famous as a…
mcq.explain: Bob Marley brought reggae to the world with messages of unity and peace.

mcq.q: Jonkanoo is a Jamaican…
mcq.explain: Jonkanoo is a Christmas-time masquerade of African origin.

mcq.q: The Jamaican national motto is…
mcq.explain: 'Out of Many, One People' reflects Jamaica's diverse roots.

meta.hook: A story from our world: Caribbean Culture

lessons.text: A story from our world: Caribbean Culture

mcq.q: A map shows…
choices: A small part of Earth from above
mcq.explain: Maps show areas from above, showing features like roads and rivers.

mcq.q: North is usually at the… of a map.
mcq.explain: By convention, north points to the top of a map.

mcq.q: A map's key explains…
mcq.explain: The key (legend) tells you what each symbol means.

mcq.q: A compass shows…
mcq.explain: A compass needle points north, helping find direction.

mcq.q: Between N and E lies…
mcq.explain: NE (north-east) is halfway between N and E.

mcq.q: The scale of a map shows…
choices: Distance on the ground vs. on the map
mcq.explain: E.g., 1cm on the map may = 1km on the ground.

flashcards.back: A drawing of an area seen from above
Ratio of map distance to real distance
A round model of the Earth

meta.hook: A story from our world: Maps & Navigation

lessons.text: A story from our world: Maps & Navigation

mcq.q: A mountain is…
mcq.explain: Mountains are tall, steep landforms — Jamaica's highest is Blue Mountain Peak.

mcq.q: A valley is a…
mcq.explain: Rivers often flow through valleys.

mcq.q: Blue Mountain Peak is Jamaica's highest point at about…
mcq.explain: Blue Mountain Peak rises to 2,256 metres above sea level.

mcq.q: Weathering is when…
mcq.explain: Rain, wind, and temperature break down rocks over time.

mcq.q: A river's mouth is where it…
choices: Meets the sea or a lake
mcq.explain: The mouth is where the river ends, often at the ocean.

mcq.q: A coral reef is made of…
choices: Tiny living animals and their skeletons
mcq.explain: Coral polyps build reefs that protect coasts and host fish.

flashcards.back: Triangular land at a river mouth
Moving of broken rock by wind/water

meta.hook: A story from our world: Landforms & Changes

lessons.text: A story from our world: Landforms & Changes

mcq.q: Weather is…
mcq.explain: Weather = short-term. Climate = long-term pattern.

mcq.q: Jamaica has a… climate.
mcq.explain: Tropical means warm year-round, with wet and dry seasons.

mcq.q: Hurricane Gilbert hit Jamaica in…
mcq.explain: Gilbert, one of the strongest Atlantic hurricanes, struck Jamaica in 1988.

mcq.q: How can we prepare for a hurricane?
mcq.explain: Stock supplies and secure the home before landfall.

mcq.q: Drought is…
choices: A long period with little rain
mcq.explain: Droughts stress crops and water supply.

mcq.q: Trade winds in Jamaica come mainly from the…
mcq.explain: North-easterly trade winds keep Jamaica cool and bring moisture.

flashcards.back: Long-term weather pattern of a place
Overflow of water onto dry land
Steady winds from the NE (in tropics)

meta.hook: A story from our world: Weather & Climate

lessons.text: A story from our world: Weather & Climate

mcq.q: Conservation means…
mcq.explain: Conservation keeps resources for the future.

mcq.q: The 3 R's are…
mcq.explain: Reduce, reuse, recycle — reduce waste and protect Earth.

mcq.q: Deforestation means…
mcq.explain: Deforestation removes forests, harming habitats and climate.

mcq.q: Water pollution can come from…
mcq.explain: Chemicals, sewage, and garbage contaminate water.

mcq.q: Jamaica's national tree is the…
mcq.explain: The Blue Mahoe is Jamaica's national tree.

mcq.q: Endangered species are…
mcq.explain: Endangered means very few left, in danger of disappearing.

meta.hook: A story from our world: Earth & Conservation

lessons.text: A story from our world: Earth & Conservation

mcq.q: The Caribbean Sea is part of the…
mcq.explain: The Caribbean is a sea within the Atlantic Ocean.

mcq.q: Most Caribbean islands were formed by…
mcq.explain: Many Caribbean islands are volcanic in origin.

mcq.q: Coral reefs protect the coast from…
mcq.explain: Reefs break waves, reducing erosion and flooding.

mcq.q: A renewable resource of the Caribbean is…
mcq.explain: Sun (and wind) is renewable — great for solar power.

mcq.q: Which is NOT a Caribbean island country?
mcq.explain: Brazil is in South America, not the Caribbean.

mcq.q: Climate change can cause…
mcq.explain: Warmer seas energise hurricanes and raise sea levels.

flashcards.back: Part of the Atlantic, surrounds many islands
Undersea ridge built by tiny coral animals
Long-term global warming and its effects
Variety of living things in an area
Living and non-living things working together

meta.hook: A story from our world: Caribbean Environment

lessons.text: A story from our world: Caribbean Environment

mcq.q: Heritage means…
choices: Things passed down from the past
mcq.explain: Heritage is the traditions, culture, and history we inherit.

mcq.q: Jamaica's heritage includes roots from…
mcq.explain: Jamaica's 'Out of Many, One People' reflects mixed heritage.

mcq.q: Devon House in Kingston is a…
mcq.explain: Devon House is a restored 19th-century mansion and popular heritage site.

mcq.q: The National Hero with 'Up you mighty race' is…
mcq.explain: Marcus Garvey inspired Pan-African pride worldwide.

mcq.q: A UNESCO World Heritage Site in Jamaica is…
mcq.explain: Declared in 2015 for its biodiversity and Maroon history.

mcq.q: The best way to keep heritage alive is to…
mcq.explain: Teaching and celebrating heritage keeps it strong.

flashcards.back: What is passed down — culture, history
UN agency for culture and heritage

meta.hook: A story from our world: Our Common Heritage

lessons.text: A story from our world: Our Common Heritage

mcq.q: If Maria is x years old and her brother is 3 years younger, how old is her brother?
mcq.explain: "3 years younger" means subtract 3 from x.

mcq.q: Which symbol means "not equal to"?
mcq.explain: The ≠ symbol means two values are different.

mcq.q: In the pattern 3, 7, 11, 15, ... what is the rule?
mcq.explain: Each term increases by 4: 3→7→11→15.

mcq.q: Solve: n + 8 = 15. What is n?
If a patty costs J and you buy 5, what is the total cost?
mcq.explain: 5 patties at J each means 5 × p = 5p.

mcq.q: Which is true?
mcq.explain: 9 is greater than 4.

flashcards.back: A letter (like x, y, n) that stands for an unknown number.
Both sides have the same value.
The two sides are not equal.
Less than — the left side is smaller.
Greater than — the left side is bigger.
The operation or change repeated to get from one term to the next.

flashcards.front: If a bus has s seats and there are 3 buses, how many seats altogether?

pairs: A letter that stands for a number
A sequence that follows a rule

meta.hook: Time to play with numbers! Patterns & Algebraic Thinking

lessons.text: Time to play with numbers! Patterns & Algebraic Thinking
